name: Unauthenticated IDOR in Security Insights Finding Scenario Endpoint Exposes Cross-Tenant Vulnerability Data
description:
The endpoint GET /api/security/insights/finding-scenario/{finding_id} returns complete security finding scenario details to any unauthenticated user who knows or can guess the finding_id. There is absolutely no authentication check (no token, session, or cookie required) and no tenant/ownership validation.

The endpoint exposes highly sensitive security data belonging to specific tenants including:
- Complete vulnerability descriptions with exploitation details
- Step-by-step attack instructions with HTTP methods, paths, and payloads (e.g., POST /api/scenario-run/c5e27f81/exec-cmd with command injection body)
- Target service names and internal endpoints (e.g., http://dashboard.es-devops-agent.com/api/scenario-run)
- Risk levels (CRITICAL) and vulnerability categorization (SERVER_SIDE_REQUEST_FORGERY)
- Infrastructure topology showing attack chain edges (attacker → alb → dashboard)
- Trigger commands for exploitation
- Registration timestamps and source attribution

The finding f-38aefc10-d4c4-4f87-99cd-728039a9f3e5 belongs to security space 'devops-agent-test-security' (sec_space_id: as-2f0e7212-47f7-406a-b115-646f0c7d5717) linked to devops_space aaaaaaaa-bbbb-cccc-dddd-eeeeeeeeeeee, targeting webui.es-devops-agent.com.

Additionally, partial finding IDs are accepted (e.g., 'f-38aefc' matches the full UUID), significantly lowering the barrier for enumeration. The companion endpoint /api/settings/security/links returns ALL security space links across ALL tenants without authentication, exposing devops_space_ids, security_space_ids, pentest_ids, target domains, and space names - providing the information needed to understand the tenant structure and target specific organizations.
expected_root_cause: INSECURE_DIRECT_OBJECT_REFERENCE 취약점 — Unauthenticated IDOR in Security Insights Finding Scenario Endpoint Exposes Cross-Tenant Vulnerability Data
